purpose: Run a bounded Mac-side brute-force search for exact WXYZTI row selectors over source-column feature terms, as a benchmark before claiming any source-native generator.
boundary: This does not close Gap A.
This searches exact selector formulas, not a source-native row/register generator.
A successful selector family is useful only as a benchmark and overfit target.
Singleton-heavy formulas are treated as lookup-like.
Mode 2 mirror remains projection-level evidence and is not promoted here.

interpretation.main_result: The bounded brute-force family found exact row selectors, but this is not a source-native generator.
interpretation.why_not_closure: Exact row-local selectors can still be disguised lookup. Gap A needs a generator that produces WXYZTI row/register structure from source, not one formula per row.
interpretation.hard_rows: The decisive rows remain the non-path and zero/preimage/stable rows.
interpretation.next_move: Audit the exact selector family for overfit, then isolate reusable subgrammar if any survives.

claim.short_form: The bounded brute-force family found exact row selectors, but this is not a source-native generator.
claim.not_closed_reason: Exact row-local selectors can still be disguised lookup. Gap A needs a generator that produces WXYZTI row/register structure from source, not one formula per row.
claim.next_problem: Audit the exact selector family for overfit, then isolate reusable subgrammar if any survives.
